# ppb-mcp

> An MCP server exposing Poor Paul's Benchmark (PPB) — real GPU inference benchmarks
> for quantized LLMs — as queryable tools.

## What this is
A FastMCP server wrapping the HuggingFace dataset `paulplee/ppb-results`
(~34,800 rows, refreshed hourly). Covers throughput, VRAM, concurrency, latency,
power draw, and model quality scores across quantized GGUF models.

## Endpoint
- Streamable HTTP: https://mcp.poorpaul.dev/mcp
- Health: https://mcp.poorpaul.dev/health

## Hardware in the dataset (current)
- Apple M4 Pro (64 GB)
- NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Ti (32 GB total, dual-card)
- NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5060 Ti (16 GB)
- NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (32 GB)
- NVIDIA GB10 (120 GB)

## Runner types — IMPORTANT
The dataset mixes two runner types that are NOT directly comparable:
- `llama-bench`            — raw single-prompt throughput (higher tok/s numbers)
- `llama-server-loadtest`  — real concurrent-user throughput (lower, more realistic)
Always filter by `runner_type` when comparing speeds across GPUs.

## Quantitative tools
- `list_tested_configs`           — all GPUs, models, quantizations in the dataset
- `query_ppb_results`             — filter raw rows by GPU/model/quant/users/backend/runner_type
- `recommend_quantization`        — best quant for a given VRAM budget and user count
- `get_gpu_headroom`              — VRAM sanity check for a specific config
- `compare_quants_quantitative`   — side-by-side TPS/TTFT comparison across quantizations
- `get_combined_scores`           — quantitative + qualitative metrics in one call

## Qualitative tools (data sparse — check has_qualitative_data flag)
- `get_qualitative_summary`       — context rot, tool accuracy, MT-Bench scores for a config
- `query_qualitative_results`     — filter qualitative rows
- `get_context_rot_breakdown`     — long-context recall by length/depth/needle type
- `get_tool_accuracy_breakdown`   — structured output accuracy metrics
- `compare_quants_qualitative`    — quality score comparison across quantizations

## Usage tip
Call `list_tested_configs` first to orient yourself on what GPUs and models are available.
Never pass the string "null" for optional parameters — omit the parameter entirely.
